identity.title: Welcome

business-model-canvas.tagline: Map an entire business model in nine blocks

value-proposition-canvas.tagline: Align customer jobs with what you offer

three-horizons-map.tagline: Sequence core businesses, emerging growth engines, and future options

bcg-growth-share-matrix.tagline: Classify a portfolio as Stars, Cash Cows, Question Marks, and Dogs

ad-lib-value-proposition.tagline: Draft a one-sentence value proposition and iterate

empathy-map.tagline: Step into your customer's head — what they think, see, say, hear, feel

jobs-to-be-done.tagline: When ___, I want to ___, so I can ___ — plus the emotional + social jobs underneath

business-model-environment.tagline: Map the four external forces shaping your business model

customer-journey.tagline: Map a customer's experience over time — find the dips, design the lifts

blue-ocean-strategy-canvas.tagline: Plot a value curve per company across competitive factors — Blue Ocean Strategy

design-criteria-canvas.tagline: Turn vision, research, and constraints into decision standards

experiment-canvas.tagline: Turn risky assumptions into measurable validation experiments

evidence-scorecard.tagline: Summarize evidence strength, learning velocity, risk reduction, and portfolio decision

innovation-culture-map.tagline: Diagnose why innovation gets stuck by mapping outcomes, behaviors, and organizational mechanisms

scenario-matrix.tagline: Build four plausible futures from two critical uncertainties

platform-ecosystem-map.tagline: Map platform sides, core interaction, network effects, governance, and monetization

ansoff-matrix.tagline: Market Penetration, Market Development, Product Development, Diversification — a 2×2 framing growth paths and risk

disruption-diagnosis.tagline: Christensen's strict 3-part test: foothold + initial inferiority + upmarket trajectory

porters-value-chain.tagline: Break the firm into 5 primary + 4 support activities to see where margin is squeezed

myProjects.pageSubtitle: Your own projects — created from a blank canvas or forked from the case library.

library.pageSubtitle: A connected path through company cases, analysis canvases, business model patterns, experiments, strategy frameworks, and source materials.

intro.title: Start from real companies and strategic judgment
intro.body: Company cases are the **application layer** of the Strategy Library: each one connects company context, canvases, and narrative. Read a case to understand the strategic question, then move into the related canvases, patterns, and frameworks.

intro.title: Put the question onto a working canvas
intro.body: Analysis canvases are the **working-tool layer** of the library. They are not attachments; they structure business models, customer value, value curves, portfolios, evidence, and future scenarios into editable workspaces.

canvasMethod.defaultTagline: Use this to structure a business or strategy question.

intro.title: Recognize reusable business structures
intro.body: Business models are the **pattern layer** of the library: they explain how companies organize revenue, cost, platforms, long tails, free models, and open innovation. Read the pattern, then return to cases to see how it works in practice.

intro.title: Use strategy frameworks to reason about choices, not just describe the present
intro.body: Strategy frameworks are the library's **lens layer**: they help you read competition, environment, organizational capability, innovation portfolios, future scenarios, and customer value. Each framework analyzes a specific object — understand what it looks at first, then open related cases to see how companies form trade-offs and action.

strategyFramework.descriptionEmpty: No description authored for this strategy framework yet.

intro.title: Trace the sources behind cases and methods
intro.body: Resources are the library's **source layer**: recommended books, articles, reports, papers, and public materials. Each entry explains why it is worth reading, which canvases / cases / methods it supports, and what questions it helps answer.

resource.descriptionEmpty: No reading note authored for this resource yet.

intro.title: Test risky assumptions before you build
intro.body: Each card is a cheap, falsifiable test of ONE risky assumption. Pick one based on **theme** (Discovery is cheaper / weaker evidence; Validation is costlier / stronger), the **risk** it tests (Desirability / Feasibility / Viability), and the **cost band** your team can afford. Every experiment lists the canvases it most often validates — claims you've already written on Value Proposition Canvas, Empathy Map, Customer Journey, BMC, etc. To run one, open the card and click "Use this experiment" — we'll spin up a fresh Experiment Canvas with the method already filled in.

experiments.useThisExperiment: Use this experiment to start a new canvas
experiments.seedFailed: Couldn't pre-fill the experiment — start the canvas blank and add the method manually.

copilot.rememberThisBrowserHint: Leave unchecked to keep it for this session only; closing the tab/browser means you'll enter it again.

createProject.hint: Enter a question, idea, material, or link first. Send keeps chatting; Create project sends the same content into a project draft.

libraryReference.placeholder: Enter the question, supporting material, or analysis direction…

projectWork.hint: Working from this project. You can add a canvas, draft a Story, or ask Copilot to analyze the next step.

composerPrompts.createProject: Create a PinGarden project draft from my images, links, and notes. Preserve visible labels/stickies from every image and turn descriptive notes into a Story.
composerPrompts.askLibrary: Based on the Strategy Library, summarize which cases, canvases, or methods are worth looking at, then ask me the single most important question.
composerPrompts.createFromReference: Based on the current reference material, turn it into a new project draft. If essential information is missing, ask me the key question first.
composerPrompts.copyProject: Based on this read-only reference project, prepare a new project draft I can copy into my workspace while preserving the most important canvas structure and insights.
composerPrompts.askReference: Explain the core question, key mechanism, industry/case background, and best follow-up questions in the current reference material.
composerPrompts.extractInsight: Summarize this discussion into a session insight card with source references and suggested project actions. Do not write project data yet.
composerPrompts.optimizeProject: Audit the current project using its existing canvases, Stories, and my long-term preferences. Find the 3-5 highest-value improvements and output a confirmable project update draft. Prefer concrete canvas sticky or Story updates over generic advice.
composerPrompts.addCanvas: Based on the current project and the material I provide, output an applicable project update draft: add or enrich the most valuable canvas and include the complete stickies to write.
composerPrompts.addStory: Based on the current project and the material I provide, output an applicable project update draft: create or update a Story connecting key canvases, insights, assumptions, and next actions.

kimi.title: Connect Kimi Code
kimi.intro: Paste your Kimi Code API key. We'll encrypt it locally and wire Kimi CLI's config for you — no terminal required.
kimi.httpIntro: Paste your Kimi API key. PinGarden Cloud uses it only for this request and never saves it to CloudBase or the PinGarden server.

skillPack.heading: PinGarden AI Strategy Skill Pack
skillPack.intro: Download the skill pack and use it directly in Claude, Code Cursor, Codex, CodeBuddy, WorkBuddy, or another AI agent. Even without the PinGarden app it works as a strategy-learning and business-canvas advisor; with the Mac app and CLI installed it can also read and write your local canvases.
skillPack.promptIntro: Drag the zip into your AI chat (or share its path), then paste this prompt:
skillPack.bundledPlaybooksTitle: Strategy methods you can learn from
skillPack.bundledPlaybooksIntro: These playbooks can guide the AI's strategy coaching. Without the PinGarden app it stays in learning/advisor mode; only a connected app can enable canvas reads and writes.
skillPack.installPrompt:
I just downloaded the PinGarden AI Strategy Skill Pack: {{filename}} (version {{version}}).

Please install it and use it in two modes:

1. Detect which AI coding agent you are (Claude, Code Cursor, Codex, CodeBuddy, WorkBuddy, or Other) and extract the zip into that agent's standard skills / rules / workflows directory. Common locations:
   - Claude: ~/.claude/skills/
   - Code Cursor: ~/.cursor/rules/
   - Codex: ~/.codex/
   - CodeBuddy / WorkBuddy: their official skill install path
   - Other: read INSTALL.md inside the zip

2. If the PinGarden app or `pingarden` CLI is not available, use learning/advisor mode: explain strategy canvases, coach me through BMC/VPC/JTBD/customer journeys, analyse cases, and suggest experiments, but do not claim you can write into the app.

3. If I have installed and launched the PinGarden Mac app, run `pingarden doctor` to verify the CLI and local server. Only after that, switch to connected app mode where you may read/write local projects, canvases, snapshots, and case forks.

4. After installation, briefly explain what each mode enables and remind me that after PinGarden app or library upgrades I can ask you to re-check and update the skill.
skillPack.installMdHint: If your AI tool is not listed above, INSTALL.md inside the zip provides a generic setup path; you can still use the pack for strategy learning without the PinGarden app.
skillPack.notReady: The skill pack was not bundled in this build. Run `pnpm build:skill-pack` before Mac packaging or cloud deployment.
